title: ask Write(*) + dontAsk mode -> the ask resolves to DENY (no prompt to be had), unlike every prompting mode

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/P6-ask-rules/e-ask-with-dontask/PROOF.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Use only the Write tool — if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.
probes.result_text:
The Write tool call was **blocked** with a permission denial. Claude Code is running in "don't ask mode," which prevents me from using the Write tool without explicit permission.

Since you instructed me not to try other tools if the call is blocked, I'm reporting the result here. The file was not created.

hypothesis: Official permission-modes doc (dontAsk section, verified 2026-07-05): 'explicit ask rules are denied rather than prompting' — dontAsk auto-denies everything that would prompt, and an explicit ask rule is by definition a prompt. So the same ask Write(*) that yields ASK under default (P6-a), acceptEdits (P6-c), and even bypassPermissions (P6-d) resolves to an outright DENY here: SDK canUseTool should NOT fire (DENIED_HARD), the exact opposite observable from a/c/d. This corrects the group README's overstatement that explicit ask rules 'apply in every mode' in the sense of prompting — in dontAsk the rule still isn't silently approved, but there is no approval opportunity either. Pairs with P5-g (dontAsk resolves protected-path asks to deny the same way).
